name: Deterministic Elden Ring Seamless Co-op selected-slot autoload on Linux Proton

description: Baseline: seed autoresearch session and measure current deterministic autoload/static-RE state

asi.hypothesis: Establish a committed autoresearch baseline around the existing no-nudge autoload implementation, static Continue/load hook evidence, and latest structured runtime telemetry without launching ER.
asi.caveat: The first scorer credited native_request_consumed from stale structured evidence plus title bootstrap even though game_save_state stayed 0. This is not a runtime-success claim; next iteration should tighten scoring to require an actual save/load state transition for 800.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix measure.sh so 800 requires native request consumption plus nonzero save/load-state transition evidence; then reinitialize the experiment baseline because the benchmark scoring semantics were corrected.

name: Deterministic Elden Ring Seamless Co-op selected-slot autoload on Linux Proton (strict no-false-positive scorer)

description: Tighten scorer so native_request_consumed requires queued request plus load-hook state transition

asi.hypothesis: Eliminate a false-positive 800 by only counting native_request_consumed when a queued direct continue request is followed by a native load hook with nonzero save/load-state transition evidence.
asi.observed_trace: continue-trace shows queued slot=9 request_save/save_request_profile, then menu_new_or_load_wrapper -> current_slot_load_67b570 ret=1 with GameMan state 0->1 and b73 cleared. Final telemetry still player_available=false/game_save_state=0, so this remains 800 not 1000.
asi.next_action_hint: Static-RE the downstream reset to slot=-1/state=0 after current_slot_load_67b570; likely need identify which menu/scheduler state or GameMan field selects the actual requested slot and keeps load progression alive.

description: Try b72-only direct continue queue via set_save_slot + save_request_profile without request_save

asi.hypothesis: Because static disassembly shows request_save(1) sets both GameMan b73 and b72, try a b72-only native queue by calling save_request_profile(1) after set_save_slot(slot), hoping the scheduler chooses continue_load_67b750 instead of current_slot_load_67b570.
asi.runtime_command: ./.auto/measure.sh with .auto/run-runtime-once=150, which delegated to .auto/runtime_probe.sh and scripts/er-smoke-driver.sh drive --launch-mode direct-protected --max-seconds 150 --max-nudges 0 --screenshot-ext jpg
asi.observed: Trace shows set_save_slot(9) then save_request_profile(1) leaves b72=1,b73=0, but no menu_continue_wrapper/continue_load/native consumption occurs within 150s. Later game code sets slot=-1 while b72 remains set. Final telemetry remains player_available=false, game_save_state=0, game_save_requested=true.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric dropped from 800 to 400: b72-only queue did not produce the previously observed native load-hook state transition.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry b72-only alone. Static RE should identify the menu-task condition that invokes menu_continue_wrapper/continue_load_67b750, or test a targeted queued call that sets b72 while preserving the title/menu task object state that Continue uses. Runtime wrapper teardown and no-pointer path worked and can be reused.

asi.runtime_command: ./.auto/measure.sh with .auto/run-runtime-once=150, delegating to .auto/runtime_probe.sh and scripts/er-smoke-driver.sh drive --launch-mode direct-protected --max-seconds 150 --max-nudges 0 --screenshot-ext jpg
asi.observed: Telemetry stayed at autoload_last_status='waiting for game scheduler tick before direct continue queue for slot 9' for 150 attempts. asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric dropped from 800 to 600; scheduler_tick gating prevented the no-overlay autoload path from queuing at all in this runtime path.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not simply gate the background poller off. If scheduler alignment is needed, add a positive handoff from the poller to the game task and prove the game task observes GameMan before blocking background writes.
